member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiException
member.summary: Exception type that is thrown when a UEI method fails

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiException.#ctor(HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiErrorType)
member.param: The error type

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiCommandException
member.summary: Exception type that is thrown when the command ID in the UEI reply message is 2

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiCommandException.#ctor(System.UInt16,System.String)
member.param: The error ID
The error message

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiCommandException.ErrorId
member.summary: The error ID decoded from the reply message

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiCommandException.ErrorMessage
member.summary: The error message decoded from the reply message

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiCommandMessage
member.summary: Base interface for a UEI command message

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiCommandMessage.TrySetFromByteArray(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Attempts to set the command's payload data from a byte array. Used when populating data on a reply message or when populating cached messages.
member.param: The payload byte array
member.returns: True if the payload is valid and was used to set the payload data; false if there was an error

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiCommandMessage.TimestampUs
member.summary: Gets the timestamp of the command. Will only be populated for reply messages or cached messages

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientCommandSendReceiveMessageAsync(HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiTransmitCommandMessage)
member.returns: If the reply command is part of the TOS UEI spec (command ID < 100), then an instance of the class associated with that command will be returned. If the reply command is client-specific (command ID >= 100) and a type was registered with for its command ID, an instance of the registered class will be returned. Otherwise an instance of UnknownCommandMessage will be returned.
member.remarks: This method can only be used to send client-specific commands (command ID >= 100)
member.exception: If the reply message has a command ID of 2

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.RegisterCustomCommandMessage``1(System.Byte)
member.summary: Registers a custom command message. UEI client DLLs must call this for any client-specific commands (for command IDs >= 100) to allow the type to be returned by and
member.typeparam: The type of the command object
member.param: The command ID
member.exception: If is less than 100 or already registered

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientConfigureUeiStreamingAsync(HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingEntry[0:,0:])
member.summary: Configures UEI streaming with the UEI client. must be called later to start streaming.

remarks.para: This API will issue a series of UEI commands (64-68) to establish the streaming setup with the UEI client.
After streaming is configured must be called to start streaming.
An error will be returned if streaming is currently running. Call before calling this API.
This will stream at the currently-set baud rate and streaming interval (4ms default). An error will be returned if the baud is too low.
Note that TOS auto-updates to the fastest baud during TOS init if the client supports UEI command 76

Note on defaulted output values for TDAU and SerialCapture (DTS). Applies to UEI streaming and DataStream APIs/GUIs:
222.2F TC will set diode temps to this when not polling temperatures (idle). Actual temperatures should never be near this hot...
223.3F
TC will set diode/Serial Capture (DTS) temps to this for each diode that's not enabled/attached (ex: RCTC3 has two unattached diodes per TDAU).
In reality, this value should not get exposed to customers, UEI for TDAUs.
Will see this for SerialCapture if a group was not assigned any SC pins.
224.4F TC will use this for SC/DTS temps over UEI that were filtered out with the SC Group filter, or if all DTS in a group were filtered (or all were a mix of filtered and stale).
225.5F TC will use this for SC/DTS temps over UEI and DataStreaming (Bulk) that were stale (not read by pattern within past 12ms).

member.param:
What values will be configured to stream from the RCTC to the UEI client.
This is a two-dimensional array. The first dimension is the index of each UEI packet's set of streaming data that the RCTC will round robin through each 4ms (default). Typically one packet per DUT, where the indexing maps to the DUT ordering in the .soc file.
The second dimension is the index of the streaming ID or static value to stream within each packet. Up to 16 streaming values per packet.
Per the UEI spec, each packet must be configured with the same number of sensor IDs and static values and in the same order, though the sensor IDs and static values can be different between packets.
Each value is an individual sensor ID or static value to stream. Static values are the initial values to stream until is called.
An error will be returned if this vector is misconfigured, as stated above, has too many packets defined, or more than 16 entries per packet.
member.returns:
The values the UEI client will stream up to the RCTC host, based on its reply to the UEI commands:
64(0x40) - Streaming Num Unique Packet Ids Request -> 65(0x41) Streaming Num Unique Packet Ids Reply
67(0x43) - Streaming Value Ids From Target Request -> 68(0x44) Streaming Value Ids From Target Reply
These IDs are client-specific. Can be empty if client isn't streaming any sensors up to the RCTC.
member.exception: If the call fails. will be set to if streaming is already running, as any new settings will not have taken effect.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientStartUeiStreamingAsync
member.summary: Sends the Start Streaming UEI command.

remarks.para: Starts UEI streaming by sending the StartStreaming UEI command.
Validation note: It may take a few ms for the UEI status bits in the bulk packet to reflect the streaming status after this call, if using ().
This API must be be called after to start streaming.
This API must also be called after to re-start streaming.
This API allows configuring UEI streaming once and providing a quick way to Stop/Start streaming afterwards.
must be called before this API.
No exception will be thrown if streaming is already running.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientStopStreamingAsync
member.summary: Sends the Stop Streaming UEI command.

remarks.para: Stops any active UEI streaming by sending the StopStreaming UEI command.
It may take a few ms for the UEI status bits in the bulk packet to reflect the stopped status after this call, if using .

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientGetSwVersionAsync(System.Byte)
member.summary: Sends a UEI command to request the SW version of the attached UEI client.

remarks.para: Sends the Get SW Version UEI command.

member.param: Which subsystem in the UEI client to retrieve the SW version of. Typically 0. See the UEI SW guide for more info.
member.returns:
The version information returned by the client. Only and may be populated, depending on how how the UEI client FW replied.
Newer UEI clients may also return and . maps to version_patch and maps to version_build in the UEI reply, per the UEI spec

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientGetSupportedBaudsAsync
member.summary: Sends the Get Supported Bauds UEI command.

remarks.para: The Get Supported Bauds command is also sent during TOS initialize for UEI client discovery to auto-update to the fastest compatible baud.

member.returns: The list of bauds supported by the attached UEI client. This is the list returned by the UEI client. This list will be sorted in highest to lowest descending order.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientTargetIdTestAsync(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Sends the Target ID UEI command and compares against the expected UEI ID, to ensure the correct device is connected.

remarks.para: The Target ID command is also sent during TOS initialize for UEI client discovery.

member.param:
The expected ID of the attached UEI client. UEI IDs are initially assigned by the TOS team to ensure they're unique.
See uei_client_ids.h for the list of client IDs
Pass in 0 to skip the target ID check and return success if the client simply replied.
member.exception: If the call fails. will be set to if the expected UEI ID and target UEI ID mismatch.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientGotoSafeStateAsync
member.summary: Sends the UEI SafeState command to the UEI client.

remarks.para: The Safe State UEI command is also sent automatically by TOS as needed, currently only during a ShutdownTester shutdown Safe State.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientSendDutMapAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiDutInfo})
member.summary: Sends the UEI DUT map to the UEI client.
member.param:
The DUT map to send. row & column are 1-based numbers.
One entry per DUT. Index in IEnumerable maps 1:1 to 0-based Package DUT index.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientAdjustControlSetValueAsync(System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte,HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiAdjustControlSetValueAction,HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiControlSetValueId,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adjusts a control set value in the UEI client's control set.
member.param: The DUT index to modify the CS value of. This is the Pkg-level DUT index and does not correlate to different IPs for intraDUT. This maps to the DUT ordering in the .soc file.
The thermal zone index to modify the CS value of.
The Control Set index to modify. Set to all Fs for active CS or global setting. Exact behavior can be client-defined.
What action to take on the specified CS value.
Which field in the client's control set to adjust.

If == , set to this new value. If == , adjust existing value by specified amount. Clients can treat this as a float if needed: value = BitConverter.SingleToInt32Bits(float_delta) Client FW side (C++): float val = *(float*)(&uei_rx_data)

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientSetUeiStreamingWeightsAsync(System.UInt32,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.UInt32})
member.summary: Can be used to assign a weight for each configured UEI streaming value.

remarks.para: Can be used to assign a weight for each configured UEI streaming value.
The UEI client can use these weights to put more emphasis on specific streaming values for any values that are summed / averaged by the client.

For example if streaming index 0 and 1 are TDAU readings and you want a weighted average of these two, you could, for example, put a weight of 5 for the first, and 8 for the second to put more emphasis on the second TDAU reading. Then, on the client side:

Not all configured streaming values are required to have a weighted value; this is fully controlled by the client's needs and what values it needs to sum. If values that need to be summed aren't contiguous in the stream, can just set 0 for the other values and ignore them in the summing.
Some clients may require all values add up to 100, to be used more as a percentage, but this is not required.
Client FW is responsible for any div by 0 checks.
Set weight values at once for all values that are or will be configured to stream; cannot be set piecemeal.

member.param: which UEI packet index (often times the DUT index) to apply the weights to. See for more info.
A list of weights to apply to the configured UEI streaming values. Maps 1:1 to the configured streaming values. Can be applied before or after streaming is configured.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientSendHostInfoAsync
member.summary: Can be used to inform the UEI client of host-specific details, such as TOS version and RCTC type.

remarks.para: This helps keep TOS generic for all clients, allowing the client to make any adjustments necessary based on what TOS is running, since client FW versions don't necessarily rev with TOS SW / HW.
To improve backwards compatibility if this command is appended to in the future, it's recommended that the client does not return an error if the command payload is longer than expected.
This UEI command is also sent during TOS initialize starting TOS 3.10.0.0

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.GetCurrentUeiBaudAsync
member.summary: Call this API to get the current UEI baud rate.

remarks.para: Gets the current baud rate of the RCTC UEI
This API gets the value from the background data and does not directly communicate to the RCTC FW or UEI client FW.
If UEI comms have already been established, this will also be the rate the UEI client is at.
Note that the actual baud returned may be slightly less than the baud rate it was set to, as this API reflects the actual baud rate vs the desired baud rate.
However, there is only a 5% max difference between desired and actual bauds.
If the baud was recently updated, it may take a few milliseconds for that value to be reflected by this API.

member.returns: The current baud rate of the RCTC

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UpdateRctcUeiStreamingStaticValuesAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.UInt32,System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.UInt32,System.Int32}})
member.summary: Call this API to update the static values on-the-fly after streaming has started.

remarks.para: Use to first configure the static and sensor values and start UEI streaming.
Unlike sensor values, static values can be updated on-they-fly while streaming is enabled, but they need to be initially configured up-front before streaming starts.
See note on
This communicates only with the RCTC FW and does not issue any UEI commands.
This API will enforce a small delay if less than 250uS has elapsed since the previous call. This helps prevent spamming the RCTC FW with commands.
Will have no real affect if calling this before or after streaming is stopped.

member.param:
The new static values to stream.
This is a dictionary of dictionaries. The outer dictionary is the UEI packet index.
The inner dictionary describes which static value index(es) to update and what new static value(s) to use.
Can only update static value indexes that were previously configured as static values with .
Can update some or all of the static values at once.
An error will be returned if this map is misconfigured, as stated above, or has too many packets defined or more than 16 entries per packet.
4-byte floating point values can be streamed as well, though the data needs to be copied in without casting. If 'sensorVal' is the float to send: var staticValue = BitConverter.SingleToInt32Bits(sensorVal) The UEI client FW would need to do the same when interpreting the received value:
const float value = *(float*)&ueiRxPacket.ueiUint32RxValue; //C-style syntax

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.SetRctcUeiStreamingIntervalAsync(System.UInt32)
member.summary: This sets the UEI streaming interval rate the RCTC will send streaming data to the UEI client.

remarks.para: Default is 4ms, which is also the max rate. Can call this before UeiClientConfigureAndStartUeiStreaming() to use a slower rate.
This does not communicate to the RCTC FW or UEI client.

member.param:
The UEI streaming interval to set the RCTC FW to when streaming to the UEI client.
Must be multiple of 4ms.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.UeiClientIsConnectedAsync
member.summary: Returns True if a UEI client is attached, by checking the UEI alive sideband line.
member.remarks: This does not communicate to the RCTC FW or UEI client.
member.returns: true if UEI client is attached, false if UEI client is not attached or on error.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.WaitForUeiStatusBitStateAsync(System.UInt32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Waits for the specified status bits to be set or cleared.

remarks.para: This will timeout after 100ms
This does not communicate to the RCTC FW or UEI client.
Useful for validation and unit testing.
For consistency, it's best to call with only one bit set at a time.

item.term: Bit is set if UEI client is attached.
Bit is set if UEI client is holding the fault line active.
Bit is set if UEI client is currently resetting (Hard reset UEI sideband line held active).
Bit is set if UEI streaming is currently running.

member.param: true to wait for the bit to be set, false to wait for the bit to be cleared.
member.exception: If the call fails. will be set to on timeout.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.GetLatestCachedUeiCommandPacketsAsync(System.UInt32)
member.returns: The cached transmitted/received commands. If a cached command is part of the TOS UEI spec (command ID < 100), then an instance of the class associated with that command will be returned. If a cached command is client-specific (command ID >= 100) and a type was registered with for its command ID, an instance of the registered class will be returned. Otherwise an instance of UnknownCommandMessage will be returned.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiSdk.SubscribeToUeiStreamingPacketsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: This API creates a handle that subscribes to the TX/RX UEI data sent from the RCTC card every 4ms and returns an object that can be used to access the cached data.

remarks.para: This does not communicate to the RCTC FW or UEI client.
The UEI data is sent out (and received) through the UEI port to the attached UEI client.
The TC FW also caches this data every 4ms and sends it to SiteC, which can be used for debug, etc. This API is used to read this cached UEI data.
Take care to call when finished with subscription. This will unsubscribe.
It's recommended to call automatically by wrapping the code in an block
See example on .

member.param:
Name to associate the subscriber with. Must not be empty.
It's recommended to use a meaningful name for readability and logging purposes.
The name is case-sensitive.
Different users / threads can pass in unique names to get their own copy of the data.
member.returns: An object that can be used to get the latest cached streaming packets for the subscription
member.remarks: The returned object implements . Calling on the object will unsubscribe.
member.exception: If the call fails. will be set to if an entry already exists for .

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiStreamingPacketSubscription
member.summary: Represents a subscription to the transmitted and received UEI data sent from the RCTC card

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiStreamingPacketSubscription.GetLatestCachedUeiStreamingPacketsAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.UInt32},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.UInt32})
member.summary: Retrieve the latest cached values of the tx and rx UEI streaming data for this subscription.

remarks.para: This does not communicate to the RCTC FW or UEI client.
This API uses the name passed to and retrieves the UEI data sent and received over UEI every 4ms since the subscribe call or since the previous call to this API.
Note that if the calling process retrieves the data faster than 250Hz cadence, this API will return empty data.
Call this API faster than every 5s after the subscribe API to prevent the internal buffer from wrapping over the oldest data.
Each call to this API will flush the internal cache for the subscription's handle.
Note it may take up to 8ms for the first tx packet to send to the UEI client from RCTC.

Streaming values are all stored in a uint32_t to store the raw data. If the data was sent by the FW in some other form, like floats, can cast it back. ex:
value = BitConverter.SingleToInt32Bits(txPackets[0][0].Values[0]);

member.param:
List of which UEI TX streaming packet(s) to read. How many packets there actually are available depends on how UEI streaming was configured by the user. (Typically 0-15)
if any packet indexes in the list weren't configured to stream over UEI, the returned data for those packets will be defaulted.
Can pass in an empty list (see example above) to request all UEI packets that were configured.

Same as but for the RX UEI packets, as sent from the UEI client to the RCTC.
NOTE: The *most recent* packet is the *first* element in each vector. (reverse order)
There will be one entry in the array per 4ms. If the UEI rx interval was set higher, entries will be duplicated until the next interval.
Each Contains the number of entries that the RCTC was configured to stream per packet during .\n
member.returns:
A tuple with the transmitted and received packets:
TransmittedPackets is a dictionary of UEI packet index to an array of UEI tx packets sent for each packet index. These are the entries sent from the RCTC to the UEI client.
The dictionary key is the packet index. Usually 0 for DUT0, 1 for DUT1, etc., depending on how the UEI packets were configured.
The array for each is the full set of packets that were sent for that packet index since was called or the last call to this API.
NOTE: The *most recent* packet is the *first* element in each vector. (reverse order)
There will be one entry in the array per (4ms * num packets configured). If the UEI tx interval was set higher, entries will be duplicated until the next interval.
Each Contains the number of entries that the client FW was configured to stream to the host.
ReceivedPackets is the same as txPacketIndexes but for the RX UEI packets
member.exception: If the call fails. may be set to if no data was returned, perhaps if calling faster than every 4ms, or if called immediately after subscribed.

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiTransmitCommandMessage
member.summary: Interface for a UEI transmit command message

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiTransmitCommandMessage.ToByteArray
member.summary: Convert the command's payload to a byte array

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.IUeiTransmitCommandMessage.CommandId
member.summary: Returns the command ID for this command

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiAdjustControlSetValueAction
member.summary: Enum describing the different actions that can done for the value passed to

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiControlSetValueId
member.summary: Enum describing the different value IDs that can be passed to . Client-specific values start at 5000.

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiDutInfo
member.summary: The row and column information for a DUT

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiDutInfo.#ctor(System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: The row and column information for a DUT

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiErrorType
member.summary: Enum representing different error types for UEI calls

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiErrorType.Unknown
member.summary: The call returned an error code that does not correspond to any enum value
member.remarks: Note that the integer value of this enum value aligns to TC_SUCCESS in the C++ API. Since this enum is only used to indicate an error type in an exception, there is no TC_SUCCESS equivalent.

summary.para: Message originated from client, reply timeout, or wrong reply msg
Client fault line set, client resetting, client disconnected...
Client replied with SLIC_SIMPLE_CMD_COMM_ERROR_REPLY. See for error details
Client replied with wrong or unexpected reply ID
Sent by the virtual UEI client as the error payload ID when it receives the test error UEI command
Thermal diodes (from the TDAU card) sent as floats. All temperatures sent are in Celsius.
RCTC2/HBI TDAU card 0. Start for HDMT RCTC2, HBI

remarks.para: NOTE on defaulted output values for TDAU (and SerialCapture (DTS)). Applies to UEI and DataStream APIs/GUIs:

list.item: 222.2 - TC will set diode temps to this when not polling temperatures (idle).

summary.para: RCTC2/HBI TDAU card 1. Last valid TDAU card for RCTC2
Thermal diodes (from the TDAU card) sent as signed 32-bit integers. (temp[C] x 10) + 640
Sent as float, sum of enabled rails, multiplied by sum_scale. Rails enabled by setting PowerScale per rail

remarks.para: NOTE: PowerSums are managed at the package level in TCFW. So for intraDUT use the zones from the package-level DUT (DUT00 for non mDUT)
Zone 0 same as PowerSumDut*
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0

summary.para: Sent as signed 32-bit integer * 100, sum of enabled rails, multiplied by sum_scale. Rails enabled by setting PowerScale per rail

remarks.para: Zone 0 same as PowerSumIntDut*
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0

summary.para: Sent as float, sum of enabled rails. Rails enabled by setting PowerScale per rail, not mult. by sum_scale

remarks.para: Zone 0 same as PowerSumUnscaledDut*
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0

summary.para: Sent as signed 32-bit integer * 100, sum of enabled rails. Rails enabled by setting PowerScale per rail, not mult. by sum_scale

remarks.para: Zone 0 same as PowerSumUnscaledIntDut*
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0

summary.para: Sent as float, sum of enabled rails, regardless of power_scale or sum_scale factors

remarks.para: Zone 0 same as RawPowerSumFullDut*
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0

summary.para: Sent as signed 32-bit integer * 100, sum of enabled rails, regardless of power_scale or sum_scale factors

remarks.para: Zone 0 same as RawPowerSumFullIntDut*
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0
NOTE: RCTC2 and HBI only support Zone 0
Added in TOS 3.10.0 for DTS/SerialCapture
Note RCTC behavioral difference:

item.para: Same system-wide FPGA-based uS timer. Used to better synchronize SensorCollection/DataStream data across instruments.
Wraps after ~71.5 minutes and is reset on TOS init and ScopeShot start
This is not a system-wide timer on RCTC2, starts at FPGA load, does NOT reset on ScopeShot start, wraps after ~71.5 minutes, and is not the same timer used for SensorCollection/DataStream samples
For RCTC2, this is only precise to 10uS

remarks.para: This is the same timer exposed in HdmtApi::GetRcHwSyncTimer() for all RCTC types

summary.para: Up to 18 DUTS, up to 8 groups per DUT, up to 16 SC/DTS sensors per group
SC groups must be made with SC PinGoups and associated with .scgm files in TOS
See Serial Capture HdmtApis and Sample TPs for more details.

remarks.para: NOTE: In intraDUT, DUT00 represents the package-level. IP level starts at DUT01
NOTE on defaulted output values for TDAU and SerialCapture (DTS). Applies to UEI and DataStream APIs/GUIs:

list.item: 222.2 - TC will set diode temps to this when not polling temperatures (idle).

item.para: 223.3 - TC will set diode/Serial Capture (DTS) temps to this for each diode that's not enabled/attached (ex: RCTC3 has two unattached diodes per TDAU).
In reality, this value should not get exposed to customers, UEI for TDAUs.
Will see this for SerialCapture if a group was not assigned any SC pins

list.item: 224.4 - TC will use this for SC/DTS temps over UEI and DataStreaming that were filtered out with the SC Group min/max filters, or if all DTS in a group were filtered (or all were a mix of filtered and stale).
225.5 - TC will use this for SC/DTS temps over UEI and DataStreaming (Bulk) that were stale (not read by pattern within past 12ms).

remarks.para: NOTE: In intraDUT, DUT00 represents the package-level. IP level starts at DUT01

summary.para: SerialCapture/Digital Temperature Sensor group average/max sent as integer (value x 100)
Up to 18 DUTS, up to 8 groups per DUT, up to 16 SC/DTS sensors per group
SC groups must be made with SC PinGoups and associated with .scgm files in TOS
See Serial Capture HdmtApis and Sample TPs for more details.

remarks.para: NOTE: In intraDUT, DUT00 represents the package-level. IP level starts at DUT01

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingData
member.summary: Record for a UEI streaming packet

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingData.#ctor(System.UInt64,System.UInt32[])
member.summary: Record for a UEI streaming packet

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingEntry
member.summary: Struct that represents either a sensor ID () or a static value

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingEntry.SensorId
member.summary: Gets the sensor ID if the entry contain one; otherwise returns null

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingEntry.StaticValue
member.summary: Gets the static value as an integer if the entry contain one; otherwise returns null

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingEntry.op_Implicit(HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiSensorId)~HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingEntry
member.summary: Implicitly create a for a sensor ID from a
member.param: The source value

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingEntry.op_Implicit(System.Int32)~HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingEntry
member.summary: Implicitly create a with a static value from an integer
member.param: The source value

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingEntry.op_Implicit(System.Single)~HdmtOs.Sdk.Uei.UeiStreamingEntry
member.summary: Implicitly create a with a static value from a float
member.param: The source value
member.remarks: The float is re-interpreted as an integer
